meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits for Freelancers: Scaling Your Dev Workflow
content.seo_description: Stop struggling to scale. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build faster, impress clients, and automate your dev process.
content.answer_block: To scale your freelance business, abandon manual setup. Use a SaaS starter kit to automate authentication, database schemas, and billing. By combining Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you can reduce development time by 70%, allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS products consistently and predictably.
content.intro:
The freelance graveyard is littered with projects that never made it past the MVP stage because the developer was buried in boilerplate. Every time you start a new project, you spend thirty hours setting up Auth, Stripe webhooks, and database migrations. It's not just tedious; it's a bottleneck that prevents you from scaling your income and your impact.

But what if you stopped building from scratch? By adopting a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from a manual grind into a repeatable assembly line. Today, we bridge the gap between 'struggling solo dev' and 'efficient agency partner' using the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code.

steps.title: Architect the Foundation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your project structure. This creates a high-level blueprint that ensures your tech stack is scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable Next.js 15 project structure including Tailwind CSS, Prisma ORM, and NextAuth configuration.

steps.title: Configure Identity Management
steps.description: Auth is the biggest time-sink. Automate the integration of OAuth providers to ensure your client's user data is secure without writing custom logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure NextAuth with Google and GitHub providers, including a custom user model in Prisma.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "setup auth providers and update schema.prisma"

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel for subscriptions. Use Claude Code to inject the billing lifecycle into your starter kit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler to sync subscription status with the database users table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "create stripe webhook and subscription management API routes"

steps.title: Rapid UI Component Injection
steps.description: Use your starter kit's design system to build dashboards in minutes rather than days.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive SaaS dashboard sidebar and header using Shadcn UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "build dashboard layout with responsive sidebar and user profile dropdown"

steps.title: Automated Testing and Deployment
steps.description: Ensure your project is production-ready by automating build checks and deployment configurations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for linting, testing, and deploying to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "create CI/CD pipeline for Vercel deployment"

pro_tips: Always keep your starter kit repo as a private template; never build directly inside your template folder.
Use Claude Code's 'commit' feature to generate semantic commit messages based on your actual code changes.
Standardize your database migration naming convention in your starter kit to avoid conflicts across multiple client projects.
Inject a 'Feature Flag' system early; it allows you to deploy unfinished code without breaking production.
Prioritize 'Configuration as Code'—if you find yourself typing a command twice, write a script for it in your kit's /scripts folder.
content.next_steps: Clone your newly structured starter kit, push it to a private GitHub repository as a template, and start your next client project with a 10-hour head start.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on boilerplate and start delivering value that keeps clients coming back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
